ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for Indian wheat-flour halwa; A0FKP (cereal-based sweet confection) used as closest parent analogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for halwa as a distinct food concept; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of halwa is approximately 80 g, equivalent to a small dessert portion served at festive occasions or as a snack.

nutrient_source.title: Halwa — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, dietary fibre, calcium, and iron sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Halwa (F11090019). Sodium reported as -1 in source (data not available). Saturated fat and sugar estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for wheat halva (USDA SR Legacy #19187) as the closest approved secondary source, with adaptation documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; semolina/wheat-based sweet confection analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Indian wheat-flour halwa found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 65 estimated from the closest approved analogous food: semolina-based sweet pudding / wheat halva entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 52.0) / 100 = 33.8, where carbohydrate per 80 g serving = 65.0 × 0.80 = 52.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (estimated 38 g/100 g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; saturated fat from ghee (~5.5 g/100 g) contributes to arterial stiffness; sodium data unavailable but traditional preparation may include added salt.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit halwa to very small occasional portions (≤40 g) and monitor overall daily sugar and saturated fat intake, as the high sugar load promotes insulin-mediated fluid retention and the ghee-derived saturated fat may impair vascular compliance.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 65 g/100 g with estimated sugar 38 g/100 g and a medium-high GI (~65) produces a very high glycaemic load per serving (GL ≈ 33.8), far exceeding safe limits for blood glucose management in type 2 diabetes.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Halwa should be avoided or consumed only in very small portions (≤20 g) on rare special occasions under medical supervision, as its high sugar content and elevated glycaemic load pose a severe postprandial glucose spike risk for individuals with type 2 diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from ghee (estimated 5.5 g/100 g) raises LDL cholesterol; high sugar content (estimated 38 g/100 g) promotes hypertriglyceridaemia and endothelial inflammation relevant to atherosclerosis risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should treat halwa as an occasional indulgence only, keeping portions to ≤40 g, as the combination of ghee-derived saturated fat and high free sugar contributes to dyslipidaemia and vascular inflammation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar (~38 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6); saturated fat from ghee (~5.5 g/100 g) further amplifies systemic inflammation relevant to RA disease activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit halwa, as its high sugar and saturated fat content are both established drivers of pro-inflammatory cytokine activity that can worsen joint inflammation and RA flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Halwa (also spelled halva or halvah) is a traditional South Asian and Middle Eastern sweet confection widely consumed in Singapore's Indian community. The Singaporean version is typically made by roasting wheat flour (atta) or semolina (sooji) in ghee (clarified butter) until golden, then combining it with sugar syrup and flavouring with cardamom, and finishing with a topping of dried fruits such as raisins, cashews, or almonds. The result is a dense, moist, fudge-like cake with a rich, buttery sweetness and a warm spiced aroma. Halwa is a festive food commonly served at Deepavali, Eid celebrations, weddings, and religious ceremonies across Singapore's Indian and Malay communities. Nutritionally, halwa is energy-dense at approximately 345 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content of 65 g/100 g (of which an estimated 38 g is sugar) and 9 g fat per 100 g (predominantly saturated fat from ghee). Dietary fibre is moderate at 3.5 g/100 g. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 65 (medium-high), yielding a very high glycaemic load per standard 80 g serving (~33.8). Calcium is notably high at 118 mg/100 g. Halwa is not suitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes due to its very high sugar and glycaemic load, and should be consumed only occasionally and in small portions by those managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis.
common_user_queries: What is the GI of halwa?
What is the difference between halwa and halva?
How much sugar is in halwa?

confidence_reason: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (F11090019) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and sugar estimated from USDA SR Legacy wheat halva entry as the closest approved secondary source. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous semolina/wheat sweet confection entries. Sodium is -1 (not reported in HPB source). Overall confidence is high given the primary HPB source match and transparent secondary estimation.
notes: sodium_mg is set to -1: sodium data for this halwa preparation is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F11090019) as of 2026-04-16. Traditional halwa preparations may contain trace added salt but no reliable published figure is available from HPB or ICMR-NIN for this specific product. monounsaturated_fat_g (2.5) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.5) estimated from USDA SR Legacy #19187 (wheat halva) as the closest approved secondary source; these are approximate and should be verified against a direct laboratory analysis. trans_fat_g set to -1: not reported in HPB source; traditional ghee-based preparations are expected to contain negligible industrial trans fats but this is unconfirmed. potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g all set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this traditional Indian halwa preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or ICMR-NIN as of 2026-04-16. sugar_g (38.0 g/100 g) and saturated_fat_g (5.5 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy wheat halva (SR Legacy #19187) as the closest approved secondary source; these values are consistent with the recipe description (wheat flour, sugar, ghee). The dietary_tags in the input included 'low_sodium' which cannot be confirmed given sodium_mg is -1; this tag has been omitted from the output. The input dietary_tag 'high_fibre' is borderline (3.5 g/100 g meets some definitions); retained in classification context only. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for this specific halwa preparation is not available in the University of Sydney GI Database. Aliases consistency self-check performed: all string values in aliases_by_language (english: Halwa, Halva, Halvah, Indian Halwa, Wheat Halwa, Atta Halwa, Sooji Halwa, Semolina Halwa, Indian sweet cake; chinese: 哈尔瓦, 哈爾瓦; malay: Halwa; tamil: அல்வா; hindi: हलवा; romanized: Halwa, Alvaa, Halwaa) are confirmed present verbatim in the flat aliases array.
